# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Pipeline
on: [push]  # or pull_request (default) -- this event is triggered when you push to the repo; see other triggers in action events below     repository_dispatch, release (https://github.com/${{ github.event.client_payload.action }}/...) for more details
jobs:   
  build-deploy:   # job name used by this workflow when triggered manually or on `on` event triggers       jobs can also be specified in matrix style, ie  `matrix: [..]`     to run the same actions across multiple agents (see https://docs.github.com/en/actions#using-job-configuration-with-a-matrix)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest  # or an OS (iOS / macOS), see options below for what's available           os can be specified in matrix style, ie  `[self-hosted runners]`     to test the workflow on which version of runner(s) it should use
    steps:       // Array defining jobs runs that trigger a job         tasks (actions/checkout@v2 for fetching repository as build artifact or using .github/workspace elsewhere in your repo  # Alternatively, you can specify any bash command here.      For more information on available GitHub Actions workflow actions and runner images see: https://docs.github.com/en/actions     - name: Checkout code
    uses:        // Using github-script is one way to run scripts in the context of this action  # The repository script file contains a single newline separated list which gets expanded by `yq` at runtime before using it below      repoUrl=https://github.com/user_name/repo_name
    with:        // These are inputs for your GitHub Action workflows - you can get input from outside the action invocation via this field       # In a job step  use .env.* (iOS / macOS), see options below          key1=${{ github.event.client_payload._token }}
